children.label: B
children.text: by railroad and water, when the transportation is under common control, management, or arrangement for a continuous carriage or shipment.

children.label: A
children.text: a State and a place in the same or another State as part of the interstate rail network;

children.label: B
children.text: a State and a place in a territory or possession of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: a territory or possession of the United States and a place in another such territory or possession;

children.label: D
children.text: a territory or possession of the United States and another place in the same territory or possession;

children.label: E
children.text: the United States and another place in the United States through a foreign country; or

children.label: F
children.text: the United States and a place in a foreign country.

transportation by rail carriers, and the remedies provided in this part with respect to rates, classifications, rules (including car service, interchange, and other operating rules), practices, routes, services, and facilities of such carriers; and

the construction, acquisition, operation, abandonment, or discontinuance of spur, industrial, team, switching, or side tracks, or facilities, even if the tracks are located, or intended to be located, entirely in one State,

children.label: i
children.text: has the same meaning given that term by ; andsection 5302 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: includes a person or entity that contracts with the local governmental authority to provide transportation services; and

children.label: B
children.text: the term “public transportation” means transportation services described in that are provided by rail.section 5302 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: public transportation provided by a local government authority; or

children.label: B
children.text: a solid waste rail transfer facility as defined in , except as provided under sections 10908 and 10909 of this title.section 10908 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the representation of employees for collective bargaining; and

children.label: iii
children.text: employment, retirement, annuity, and unemployment systems or other provisions related to dealings between employees and employers.

children.label: B
children.text: The Board has jurisdiction under sections 11102 and 11103 of this title over transportation provided by a local governmental authority only if the Board finds that such governmental authority meets all of the standards and requirements for being a rail carrier providing transportation subject to the jurisdiction of the Interstate Commerce Commission that were in effect immediately before . The enactment of the ICC Termination Act of 1995 shall neither expand nor contract coverage of employees and employers by the Railway Labor Act, the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974, the Railroad Retirement Tax Act, and the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act.January 1, 1996
